summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Traefik v1.7.34's entrypoint redirect middleware evaluates Go template syntax injected into the request path. A raw HTTP request with {{.Request.Referer}} in the path causes server-side template evaluation, and the Referer header value appears in the redirect Location response header.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the crafted request path containing Go template syntax to be evaluated by the server-side template engine, extracting the Referer header value and including it in the redirect Location header.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the reproduction image, started Traefik with HTTP-to-HTTPS entrypoint redirect, and confirmed that a raw HTTP request with {{.Request.Referer}} in the path resulted in the Referer header value being reflected in the redirect Location header (e.g., Location: http://localhost:443/EVOHUNT-SSTI-PROOF).
verification_method: The verify.sh script starts Traefik, then uses exploit.py to send a raw TCP HTTP request with {{.Request.Referer}} in the path and a unique proof string as the Referer header value. It checks the redirect Location header for the proof string to confirm server-side template injection.
evidence_produced_by_run: The redirect Location header contains the Referer header value, confirming template evaluation.
A unique proof string (e.g. EVOHUNT-SSTI-PROOF-2256) was extracted from the Referer header and placed into the redirect URL via template injection.
No authentication was required to trigger the template injection.
The template {{.Request.Referer}} was evaluated server-side by Go's text/template engine in applyString().
The baseline redirect (without template syntax) returns 302 as expected, confirming the redirect is active.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository (traefik/traefik) on the host, checks out the v1.7.34 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
